73: **South Africa**, runners-up in **Group A** on 4 points behind perfect **Mexico**, meet **Canada**, who advanced as **Group B**'s second seed on the same total behind **Switzerland**. Both arrive with identical 1W-1D-1L records and now headline the tournament's first knockout tie — a 12:00 PM PT meeting where extra time and penalties loom if the 90 settles nothing.
67: **Panama**, fourth in **Group L** on 0 points, face **England**, who are level with one other side in the group on 4 points with positions not yet separated. **England** need a result to confirm their place in the knockouts and push for top spot, while **Panama** — without a point this stage — have nothing to lose and every reason to throw caution aside from the first whistle.
68: **Croatia**, third in **Group L** on 3 points, take on **Ghana**, level with one other side in the group on 4 points with positions not yet separated. **Croatia** must win to keep their tournament alive and will need other results to cooperate, while **Ghana** know a draw may be enough to secure progression — making this a tense, high-stakes affair where every half-chance carries genuine knockout weight.
71: **Colombia**, top of **Group K** on 6 points, clash with **Portugal**, second in the group on 4 points — a top-two showdown that doubles as a straight fight for first place and the favourable bracket draw that comes with it. Both sides arrive in form and with momentum, and with the rest of the group watching, expect an assertive, high-intensity contest that could shape how either team approaches the knockouts.
72: **Congo DR**, third in **Group K** on 1 point, face **Uzbekistan**, fourth in the group on 0 points — modest on paper, but pride, individual form, and squad depth all remain very much on the line. Both sides have struggled for wins at this stage, and this final group outing is a genuine opportunity for fringe players to make a case ahead of whatever comes next.
69: **Algeria**, level with one other side in **Group J** on 3 points with positions not yet separated, face **Austria**, equally level on 3 points and also not yet separated — a direct shootout where the winner almost certainly books their place in the knockouts. With goal difference tight between the two, victory is the cleanest route through; a draw could leave both sides sweating on results elsewhere and opens the door to some agonising final calculations.
70: **Jordan**, fourth in **Group J** on 0 points, host **Argentina**, top of the group on a perfect 6 points — a chance for the already-qualified **Argentina** to sharpen up ahead of the knockouts, and for **Jordan** to sign off the group stage with a performance to remember. **Argentina** are unlikely to ease off entirely with a first-place finish still to cement, but **Jordan** will be motivated to send their home support away with something to cheer.
